items.title: Iran proposes suspending nuclear activities for up to five years – NYT
items.summary: According to a report from the New York Times (NYT), Iranian officials had proposed suspending its uranium enrichment for up to five years in its weekend talks with the United States (US) in Pakistan.

items.title: Asian stocks rally on US-Iran optimism, Nikkei 225 leads
items.summary: Asian stock markets add significant gains on Tuesday as comments from United States (US) President Donald Trump and Vice President (VP) JD Vance, hinting that talks with Iran were not a complete failure, have prompted demand for risk-sensitive assets.

items.title: Hezbollah says it won't comply with agreement from Lebanon-Israel talks
items.summary: Iran-backed Hezbollah has stated in the Asian session on Tuesday that it won’t accept terms from agreements between Israel and Lebanon, who are scheduled for peace talks in Washington at 15:00 GMT.

items.title: NZD/USD treads water above 0.5850 following China’s Trade Balance data
items.summary: NZD/USD inches lower after registering 0.5% gains in the previous day, trading around 0.5860 during the Asian hours on Tuesday. The pair remains subdued following the release of China's Trade Balance data for March.

items.title: RBA’s Hauser: Focused on preventing rise in inflation expectations
items.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser, speaking in a fireside chat earlier this Tuesday, warned that Australia is facing a difficult macroeconomic backdrop.

items.title: US Vice President Vance: No deal yet, but framework for grand deal exists
items.summary: US Vice President JD Vance, speaking in an interview on Fox News, struck a cautiously optimistic tone on negotiations with Iran and suggested that meaningful progress has been made even as talks have yet to deliver a breakthrough.

items.title: NZD/USD rebounds as USD weakness dominates despite geopolitical tensions
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is pushing higher on Monday, April 13, climbing back toward the 0.5880 region as broad US Dollar (USD) weakness becomes the dominant market driver.

items.title: China: Innovation-led sectors stay in focus – HSBC
items.summary: HSBC Asset Management underlines that China’s technology sector remains a key equity theme despite shifting attention to Middle East tensions. The firm points to strong performance of the Shenzhen Chinext index, supported by advanced manufacturing, green energy and semiconductors.

items.title: Asia FX: Softer start on Oil and risk – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong expect Asian FX, especially high-beta Oil importers like KRW, THB, PHP and INR, to open weaker as renewed geopolitical uncertainty supports crude and the Dollar. However, resumed Hormuz transit tempers tail risks.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar falls even as failed talks and Hormuz shutdown spark risk-off wave
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell on Monday, as markets digest headlines of failed Iran-United States (US) peace talks over the weekend and reports that US President Donald Trump moved the US Navy to close the Strait of Hormuz, a development that would typically fuel a strong safe-haven bid for the Gre

items.title: Renminbi: Limited Petroyuan prospects in energy trade – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Head of FX and Commodity Research Thu Lan Nguyen examines Iran’s proposal to charge Strait of Hormuz tolls in Renminbi and finds it unlikely to trigger a major Petroyuan shift.

items.title: Trump says Iran wants a deal as Hormuz blockade officially begins
items.summary: US President Donald Trump spoke at a press conference at the Oval Office and praised Vice-President JD Vance's work on Iran, saying that he “has done a very good job on Iran.”
